element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Simple projectile dodging and melee combat
Vertical and horizontal navigation through confined spaces
Resource management with powerful ranged option
Timing and evasion of falling projectiles
Thematic transition level with mixed hazards
Precision jumping sequences, repeated difficulty spikes
Avoidance of pit falls and aerial enemies
Final climactic boss requiring all learned skills
Optional deep content unlocking extended ending
Complex puzzle platforming with floor mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Health bar growth after defeating enough enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Transition to Stage 6 annoying platforming sequences
Entering secret Egypt stage with trapdoors
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating danger curve: early game introduces combat and basic platforming, mid-game adds precision challenges (chainsaws, spiders), late game concentrates multiple hazard types (Stage 6-7). Secret stages provide extreme difficulty spike for completionists.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Crystal balls hidden throughout stages; accessible via secret routes and hidden enemy encounters. Secret stages (Japan, Egypt) unlocked by collectibles.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden off-screen paths, optional boss encounters, concealed collectibles within standard level progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction: Stage 1 teaches basic combat (axe), basic enemies (knives, chickens), simple boss (Possessed Girl). Mansion interiors provide safe platforming tutorials.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate escalation: Stage 2 introduces projectile hazards (hands). Stage 3 adds resource management (shotgun) and introduces instant-kill hazards (chainsaws). Spider platforming in Stage 2-3 increases precision demands. Stage 5 introduces multi-phase boss (Werewolf).
difficulty_curve.late_game: Severe difficulty spike: Stage 6 described as 'really annoying platforming section' repeated three times with escalating complexity (final section adds damaging water). Stage 7 (Hell House) combines all hazard types: chainsaws, flying enemies, floor hazards, fire rooms, and final boss.
notable_spikes: Stage 3 chainsaw introduction (transition from safe combat to instant-kill platforming)
Stage 6 three-part annoying platforming sequence (difficulty concentration)
Hell House final stage (density of all hazard types)
Secret Egypt stage with trapdoors (optional extreme difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 1, 5, and 7 feature cabin/room interiors that provide visual variety and pacing breaks from outdoor platforming. These spaces are bounded and contain specific encounter types (hands throwing, screaming paintings, flying enemies).
Major bosses (Possessed Girl, Chestburster, Werewolf, Pumpkin King) are preceded by specific encounter rooms that build tension and signal importance. Sewer level serves as thematic transition.

Chains of difficult platforming challenges concentrated in specific stages (Stage 6 with three annoying sections, Hell House with multiple hazard rooms). These sequences test mastery of all learned movement skills.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 6 three-part sequence; Stage 7 holes-and-bats room

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage world uses horror/pop-culture parodies (Michael Jackson vampire, possession, werewolves, Pumpkin King, Japanese castles, Egyptian pyramids) to guide visual and enemy variety without changing core mechanics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 3 Devil Town vs Stage 5 Diamond Camp

notable_level_patterns.description: Two hidden crystal ball collectibles unlock extended ending sequences. Secret stages (Japan, Egypt) available post-completion. Extreme difficulty concentration in secret Egypt stage.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Secret Egypt stage (pyramid with trapdoors described as 'infuriating')
